subject: GRB 220624B: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 10:20:24 UT on 24 Jun 2022, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 220624B (trigger 677758829.234126 / 220624431).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 97.8, Dec = 41.6 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 06h 31m, 41d 36'), with a statistical uncertainty of 4.1 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 92.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220624431/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn220624431.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220624431/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn220624431.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2022/bn220624431/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn220624431.gif
